track_description: Exogenous fuel-efficiency improvement in gas turbine compressors driven by background fleet replacement and advances in turbine aero-thermal design (combined heat and power integration, improved seal technology, digital combustion optimisation). Approximately 12% cumulative reduction in gas own-use intensity per unit gas production by 2050, compounding at ~0.5%/yr.
evidence_summary: Background gas turbine efficiency improvement consistent with Australian upstream energy intensity data (AES 2015-2023 declining trend) and OEM turbine upgrade programmes (GE
confidence_rating: Solar Turbines). Separate from explicit electric compression pathway to avoid double-counting.
double_counting_guardrail: Hybrid synthesis: incumbent states calibrated to AES 2023-24 Table F and NGGI 2023-24 data; transition states reflect international oil and gas sector cost and performance evidence with smoothed milestone trajectories.
column19: Applies only to conventional, LDAR, and renewable-diesel states. Electric compression and integrated states are excluded — they replace gas turbines rather than improving them. Non-stacking with compressor station upgrade efficiency package.
column20: Confirm multiplier against NGERs gas facility energy intensity trend 2015-2025 to validate ~0.5%/yr assumption.

track_description: Exogenous fuel-efficiency improvement in diesel engines at remote wellsites and drilling operations driven by background fleet replacement and engine-technology advancement (Tier 4 Final emission standards, improved fuel injection, load optimisation). Approximately 10% cumulative reduction in diesel intensity per unit gas production by 2050, compounding at ~0.4%/yr.
evidence_summary: Background diesel-engine efficiency improvement consistent with NGERs upstream energy intensity data and Tier 4 Final engine standards rollout in Australian drilling fleet. Separate from explicit wellsite solar-hybrid pathway to avoid double-counting.
derivation_method: Hybrid synthesis: incumbent states calibrated to AES 2023-24 Table F and NGGI 2023-24 data; transition states reflect international oil and gas sector cost and performance evidence with smoothed milestone trajectories.
double_counting_guardrail: Applies to states retaining significant diesel use. Renewable-diesel and integrated states are excluded — they explicitly model diesel reduction and their coefficients already embed the trajectory. Non-stacking with wellsite solar-hybrid efficiency package.
review_notes: Review multiplier against NGERs diesel intensity trend for upstream gas operations 2015-2025.
